column17:
Kit Overview: High Energy Crit DPS
Saber’s kit revolves around her [Core Resonance]. 1 [Core Resonance] is considered 8 fixed energy. Saber obtains [Core Resonance] through her Skill (+3) / Enhanced Basic Attack (+2) / Ally Ultimate (+3) / Entering battle with Technique (+3). Saber has  can hold 120 excess Energy.

Upon using Saber's Ultimate, she deals 1 instance of AoE damage, then 10 additional bounce damage which also enhances her Basic Attack.

Saber's Enhanced Basic Attack deals AoE damage, Saber will deals more damage if there are less than 2 enemies. Additionally, this ability will grant Mana Burst upon use. Under Mana Burst effect, when Saber's Ultimate can fully be charged with [Core Resonance] and Skill, Saber consumes Mana Burst to immediately take action and gain 1 SP.

Saber has tremendous amount of DMG%, CDMG% and CR% self-buff.

Synergies:
✦ Supports with good damage amplification and action advance.
✦ Supports that can generate Saber's Energy.

Tips:
✦ Conserve ally Ultimates to properly time Mana Burst consumption, especially characters who can generate energy with it.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Saber is a strong all-rounded DPS, while being slightly more difficult to pilot with the various buffs she needs to keep track of and how they affect parts of her kit.

✦ Saber has tons of in battle self buffs making her much easier to build around, and make her work with various supports.
column71: Saber prefers hypercarry setups with supports who can provide her energy and/or extra turns to stack and burn Core Resonance

column59:
✦Sunday can provide Saber a lot of energy, SPs, buffs and action advance. This makes him Saber's most important priority.
✦E1 increases Ultimate DMG. Her Basic Attack, Skill now grant 1 extra [Core Resonance].
✦S1 provides huge amount of CDMG% and ATK%. Additionally, the wearer generate 10% max energy after Ultimate if their max energy is at least 300. This is a solid statstick and provide even more energy for Saber.
✦Stop at E0S1 for low investment.

column17:
Kit Overview: Crit DPS who transform into a boss
Phainon's mechanics are based around obtaining Coreflames. Methods of getting Coreflames: Being targeted by allies' or enemies' abilities or enter battles (+1) / Getting energy restored by allies' ability (+1) / Using Skill (+2) / Leaving Territory (+3).

Phainon can hold up to 15 Coreflames, and requires 12 to cast his Ultimate. After casting Ultimate, he gains 8 extra turns and enters a special Territory where all teammates become Departed (removed temporarily from the team).

In Phainon's Ultimate, he uses a resource named Scourge which replaces the function of Skill Points. He has 3 abilities in his Ultimate form : 
- [Basic Attack]: Blast attack, restores 2 Scourge,
- [Skill 1]: Makes all enemies move instantly to attack him, gains Scourge equal to the number of enemies on field, and then Counter with an AoE + Bounce attack. The counter's damage increases the more Phainon is hit.
- [Skill 2]: Consumes 1-4 Scourge to deal AoE + Bounce damage, with a big bonus multiplier when consuming 4.
On his 8th extra turn, Phainon will cast a last AoE attack and exits his Territory, returning his teammates to the field.

Synergies:
✦ Harmony units who can generate Coreflame, provide action advance, energy, and good damage amplification.
Notes:
✦ Since all allies Depart in Territory, Phainon lacks flexibility in terms of gameplay and has to rely more on his stats/investments.
✦ Phainon's team in general is very Skill Point hungry without investments such as S1 Sunday, etc.
✦ It is generally not recommended to use Phainon with a Sustainer due to how his Territory works.
Tips:
✦ If Phainon's Coreflame generation is ensured, strong damage amplification options such as Tribbie or Robin can be used.
column45:
✦ If you like a character, get them!

✦ Phainon is a great hypercarry DPS who can clear most content at ease with good investments and does not require too much skill to pilot correctly.

✦ Phainon relies on his stats and investments more than most characters in the game. As he lacks gameplay flexibility to compensate for a lack of investment or bad relic quality.
column71:
Phainon prefers Sustainless teams with supports who can effectively generate Coreflame stacks or provide extra actions for Phainon.
Phainon's team may struggle with Skill Points without proper management.

column59: Sunday and Cerydra > E2 > S1 > E1

Sunday and Cerydra are very important since they can generate Coreflame stacks, provide great buffs, and action advance for Phainon.

E2 increases Physical RES PEN, and when Phainon uses [Skill 2] with 4 Scourge, he gains an extra turn. This effectively extends his transformation and damage.

E1 increases CDMG%, and defeating enemies during transformation will increase his SPD in Territory.

S1 increases base SPD and allows attacks to ignore DEF. When the wearer uses their Ultimate they get a damage buff that is removed at the start of their turn.

column71: Not as Premium but still works
Extended Battle - If Phainon cannot finish enemies quickly

column9: Basic if you need to save

column71: The Premium - Require investments to deal with SPs

column17:
Kit Overview: Nearly Immortal Self-HP drain Blast DPS
Mydei's normal Skill depletes his HP and does not require Skill Points. For each 1% of HP lost, accumulates 1 point of Charge, at 100 Charge, consumes Charge to enter [Vendetta] state, while active, buff his Max HP while his DEF remains at 0, increasing efficiency of Charge gains, he also becomes immune to Crowd Control debuffs. When it's Mydei's turn, automatically launch a powerful attack, if 150 Charge is gained in this state, consumes it to obtain one Extra Turn and deal an even more damaging Skill attack then heal himself.

When receiving a killing blow in the [Vendetta] state, Mydei will not be knocked down, up to 3 times in battle.
Ultimate heals and deals a blast attack, taunting all enemies for 2 turns, and the primary target of the Ultimate will be the same target focused by his Enhanced Skill. This allows for some control over his Auto playstyle.
Mydei gains additional Crit Rate and Charges ratio from enemies the more HP he has, up to 8000 HP. 

Synergies:
✦ Crit DPS support units that do more than ATK buff since Mydei scales on HP.
✦ Speed or Action Advance buffers to earn more turns for HP drains.
✦ Characters that consume his HP without hindering his performance.
✦ Healer units for HP drains.
Notes:
✦ While Ultimate can help with controlling Mydei, his Auto-play playstyle might not be for everyone.
column45:
✦ If you like a character, get them!

✦ Mydei is another strong, easy to play and flexible DPS that is worth a pick up if you favor his traits, his ability to sustain damage is notable for challenge runs or more comfort in Simulated Universe-like modes.

✦ He is rather lackluster in AoE-centric battle without E1 however. 

✦ Once again, the auto-play playstyle of him should be considered if you require precision.
column71:
Mydei purely scale of HP, so his support choices are limited.
Gallagher is the recommended pick as he has high burst healing.
Put Mydei in the middle spot in case you get lucky from getting hit with Blast attacks.

column59: S1 >= E1 > E2 - Stops at E0S1 or E1S1

S1 buffs Max HP, Incoming Healing, when using Ultimate or Skill, consumes 6% Max HP for 30% DMG% to that attack, and another 30% if 500 HP is consumed. Good option for DMG enhances, the HP drains effect further buffs Charge gain rate.

E1: Enhanced Skill's multiplier increases by 30% and now attack all targets, letting Mydei work better in AoE battles.

E2: In [Vendetta] state, DMG dealt ignores 15% DEF. When he receives healing, 40% of the amount healed is converted to Charge, a good eidolon but not outstanding.

column17:
Kit Overview: Self-sufficient Counter based DPS
When Yunli gets attacked, she additionally gains 15 energy, buff self ATK% by a decent amount and launches a Blast type Counter attack back, her skill is an attack that also heals herself up. Her max Energy cost is 240, but only require 120 to use, which helps with Energy overflow,

When activating Ultimate, Yunli taunts all enemies, reduces DMG taken, resists Crowd Control Debuff and enhances the next counter, becoming [Intuit: Cull] which has great damage, then deals 6 DMG instances to random enemies, if the counter fail, automatically launches a weaker Enhanced Counter: [Intuit: Slash], this DMG count as Ultimate DMG.

Synergies:
✦ Crit DPS supporter and abilities that give Yunli energy with energy regen, Action Advance, etc.
✦ Enemies that attack often, allies that don't compete with her aggro and/or buff her aggro.

Notes:
✦ Yunli runs into risk of getting taken down if she suffers too much DMG, so helping her gain turn or pairing her with a good sustain is needed.
✦ Yunli can activate her own Counter attack but her DMG output is still dependant on getting hit often, leading to lowered DMG output if not attacked much. Enemy delay effect is bad for her.

Tips:
✦ After doing [Intuit: Slash], the next [Intuit: Slash] will be made to be [Intuit: Cull], helpful in situations where you don't get hit often or having specific plans.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Being a Counter DPS, as long as she get attacked enough, Yunli will thrives and provide great DMG output without needing specific allies, her DMG instances will let her do well into both low target and multi targets fights.

✦ In bad conditions, you can help Yunli overcome the circumstance by giving her more turns and energy regeneration so she can use her Ultimate and launch Counter attacks on her own, which luckily, you have Tingyun as a non-limited option.
column71:
Makes use of most Crit DPS support, avoid unit with delay ability, energy regen support is on high priority.
Put Yunli in the middle spot in case you get lucky from getting hit with Blast attacks

column59: S1 > E1 > E2 - Stop at E0S1 or E1S1

S1 greatly increases the wearer aggro, allowing the wearer to get hit more often, increase Crit DMG by 36%, when the wearer use Ultimate, increase FUA DMG by 36%, stacking up to 2, a high priority for vertical invest.

E1: enhanced Counter attack deals 20% more DMG, increases [Intuit: Cull] DMG instances by 3, a strong DMG boost.

E2: Counter DMG ignore 20% DEF, decent buff that only get significant if there's other DEF ignore sources.

column71: You hit us, you die - Meme team - good for Hoolay & SimUniverse modes

column17:
Kit Overview: Strong Break-Focused Blast DPS 
Firefly is a Super Break DPS excelling in ST and Blast fights. Her ultimate applies the [Complete Combustion State]: advances Firefly by 100%, boosts Weakness Break Efficiency and Speed, and converts the skill to Blast type, allowing it to implant Fire weakness, letting Firefly breaks enemies quickly and deal strong Super Break damage.

Additionally, Firefly's technique implants Fire weakness to all enemies per wave, giving her a strong head start.

Synergies:
✦ Supports unit that can help her performance, through buffs/debuffs such as Super Break enabler, BE%, Weakness Break Efficiency, Speed, Vulnerability, Defense Shred, etc.
✦ Teammates who are SP+ for Firefly's consumption.

Notes:
✦ Firefly consumes a lot of SP due to her increased Speed after using Ultimate.
✦ Firefly requires Super Break Enablers in most situations, limiting team flexibility. 
✦ Firefly struggles against enemies immune to Toughness Reduction.

Tips: 
✦ Allies should act before Firefly acts to apply buffs/debuffs and deal Toughness Reduction first.
✦ Firefly prefers SPD over ATK boots as rolling lost SPD subs reduces potential Break Effect.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Firefly is a strong DPS with a high performance floor, able to take on a variety of content; excelling at fights with break mechanics

✦ However, while she is great at striking bulky targets down, clearing multiple waves of many weak enemies isn't her strong suit, and would require the help of other units for said cases.
column71:
Fugue and Dahlia are required if you want the best performance.
Firefly can also work with various supports but the difference between her best team and the alternatives is noticable.

column59:
✦ Fugue and Dahlia provides a huge amount of utilities, DMG increase for Firefly. It is recommended to have them both
✦ E2 allows Firefly to take an extra action in [Combustion State] if she break or kill an enemy, trigger every 2 turns. This significantly increase her DMG output.
✦ S1 increases BE%, and whenever the wearer deals Break DMG, inflict the hit enemies with Break Vulnerability and lower their SPD. This LC is ok but Firefly already has good atlernatives

✦ The recommended baseline is E2S0 Firefly, Dahlia and Fugue with investments

column71: Himeko Pure Fiction enabler - Struggle with SPs

column17:
Kit Overview: Blast-focused HP Scaling Skill Crit DPS
When Jingliu uses her Skill (ST) or Ultimate (Blast), she gains 1 stack of Syzygy. When she has 2 Syzygy stacks, Jingliu enters [Spectral Transmigration] state, advance herself, gain an extra Syzygy stack and increase its cap to 4 . In this state, Jingliu gains Crit Rate and can only use Enhanced Skill.

When Jingliu uses Enhanced Skill, consumes allies' HP and a stack of Syzygy to launch a Blast attack.

Whenever allies' HP reduce, she gains Moonlight stacks, which increases Crit Damage. Additionally when allies' HP are reduced 20 times, gains 1 Syzygy stack.

Synergies:
✦ Units that can drain allies' HP to help generate Syzygy stacks.
✦ Works well with most Harmony/Nihility that provide damage amplification, action advance, energy, etc. 
Notes:
✦ Due to the sheer amount of CDMG% and CR% self-buff, Jingliu prefers supports that provide a different damage amplification such as RES PEN or debuffs. Jingliu prefers HP and potentially SPD more due to this reason
✦ In order to maintain full uptime of Spectral Transmigration state, S1 Hyacine is required.
Tips:
✦ Jingliu should use her Ultimate during [Spectral Transmigration] state to fully benefit from her self-buff. Unless energy is extremely overcapped.
✦ Since Jingliu does not consume Skill Points during [Spectral Transmigration], this allows other allies to spam their skill more often.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Jingliu is a very solid crit DPS, who can perform relatively well for most content. She especially excels in 3-enemy situations or even Single Target with E1.

✦ However, Jingliu also shares the same supports/teams with quite a few units which can cause characters overlap.
column71:
Jingliu prefers typical hypercarry setup, prioritize teammates that gives action advance and non-saturated buffs or debuffs.

Jingliu also prefers healers over shielders or tankers like Fu xuan.

column59:
Hyacine E0S1 is especially important for Jingliu as it allows her to maintain a full uptime on her [Spectral Transmigration] state on top of a solid HP buff.

Mydei S1 gives better damage increase than her own S1 and also contribute to generating Syzygy stacks

E1 increase her CDMG% and Jingliu will deal additional damage to the primary target, This makes Jingliu more potent for pure ST battles.

Hyacine E0S1 should be prioritized before anything else.

column71: Mono Ice?! - S1 Hyacine is recommended on RMC

column17:
Kit Overview:  Enhanced Basic Attack Crit DPS
Dan Heng – Imbibitor Lunae (DHIL) is a crit DPS that excels at Blast as well as ST fights. DHIL's skill allows him to enhance his Basic Attack, the more SP you use (up to 3), the more damage it deals. The attack starts at ST type, then after the enhancing it twice, it becomes a blast attack, dealing massive damage and toughness reduction.

His Ultimate, beside dealing Damage, will give him 2 scales called [Squama Sacrosancta], acting like SP for enhancing DHIL's Basic Attack, easing SP consumption.

Synergies:
✦ Due to his SP intensive kit playing him with SP+ characters is a must. 
✦ As DHIL isn't built fast generally, it’s recommended to have an action advancer or speed buffer on the team as well. He also benefits a lot from teammates that help with his energy regeneration.

Notes + Tips:
✦ When playing DHIL you should always keep an eye on your SP consumption to bring the most out of his damage
✦ Since DHIL's Basic attack has multiple enhancement stages, regulating those according to the situation can save you SP
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ DHIL is a strong Imaginary DPS to have on hand. If you have good teams for him and decent investments, he can clear content well.
column71: Typical hyper carry setup, prioritize teammate that can provide lots of SP
Premium team - Sparkle for your SP needs

column59:
DHIL’s E2 is one of the best Eidolons in the game with a big increase in overall damage when compared to E0. It advances him forward by 100% after using his ult. Going for E1 is not recommended unless it's a stepping stone to E2.

S1 enables a 3-turn Ult rotation, is however not a massive increase to his damage overall. (~6-7%)

Getting his premium teammates like Sparkle or Sunday E0S1 would likely be more efficient than going for DHIL's own vertical investment.

column71: Another Premium team ( S1 Sunday and Gallagher for extra SP comfort)
SP mukbang (Require S1 Sunday and careful SP planning)
The Astral Express Trio - Meme team

column17:
Kit Overview: HP scaling DPS who spam abilities.
When Blade uses his skill, he consumes his HP and enters [Hellscape] state. In this state, he deals more damage; increase the chance of getting hit; and enhances his Basic Attack, which will consume his HP when used and deals Blast damage. His Enhanced Basic Attack will not generate SP and he will exit [Hellscape] state after 3 turns.

When Blade's HP reduces or he takes damage, he gains 1 Charge, when he obtain 5 Charge, Blade unleashes an AoE FUA attack, gains 15 energy and heals himself.

When Blade uses his Ultimate, he sets his HP to 50% of his max HP and deals Blast damage based on his max HP and the tallied HP that he lost during the battle.

Synergies:
✦ Blade works well with most Nihility/Harmony units as long as they give good damage amplification, action advance, etc.
✦ Prefers supports who can drain his HP for more charge and can synergize with units who can drain allies HP (Usually not recommended).

Notes:
✦ Blade can go slow or fast, depending on what supports you're using.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Blade is a very consistent and good crit DPS, he can works for most content.

✦ However, Blade also shares the same supports/teams with quite a few units which can cause characters overlap.
column71:
His damage scales on his HP now, instead of being mixed like pre-buff. All generalist supports still work. 
Put Blade in the middle spot in case you get lucky from getting hit with Blast attacks

column59:
E1 allows Blade's Enhanced Basic Attack and Ultimate to deal additional damage based on 150% of his lost tallied HP.

S1 is a good stat stick with decent effects for Blade, however he has great alternative options.

column71: Action Hypercharge for that HP drains

column9: Less frequent SP usage than average DPS
E spam until the game tell you to use A

column17:
Kit Overview: Counter based DPS
✦ When Clara gets hit, Svarog will immediately launch a ST Counter Attack to the enemy that hit her, once per hit. This attack is considered a FUA. The enemy that is hit by her counter will gain Mark of Counter, and enemies with the Mark will take extra damage from her Skill. The mark will be removed after Skill is used

✦ Upon using her Ultimate, Clara will take less damage, increase her aggro, and 2 of Svarog's counters will be enhanced. Enhanced counters deal much greater damage and hit adjacent enemies. Enhanced counters can be launched when any ally gets hit.

Synergies:
✦ Supports that can provide her energy or extra turns for ready access to her ultimate 
✦ Supports that can assist in her getting hit to launch counters

Notes + Tips:
✦ Svarog's Enhanced Counters last for 2 turns only. Time your ultimates to enemy turns to avoid wasting Enhanced Counters.
✦ When using Clara, she prefers to be as slow as possible to capitalize on enemy hits and actions.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Clara can be obtained from Standard banner or from losing 50/50 so you do not need to specifically aim to get her.

✦ Clara is a solid DPS, who can handle both ST and AoE situations, although her performance can heavily varies due to her very passive playstyle. Use her against enemies that are aggressive and can take lots of turns, like Hoolay.
column71: Getting quick access to her enhanced counters is imperative to her damage. Using supports that can help with Ult uptime (Direct energy or giving extra turns) is preferred

column59:
Robin and Tribbie are extremely helpful for to Clara, as they both provide very strong buffs that she can take full advantage of.

Yunli's S1 gives Clara a massive FUA buff, aggro increase, and free CRIT DMG

E6 gives Clara an additional enhanced counter and has a 50% chance for her regular counters to launch on ally hits

E1 prevents Mark of Counter from being removed

column9: Consumption depends on team and eidolons
